title: Maze Graphs, Connectivity, Cycles, Components, and Trees · Behavior Case: Tree Invariants
summary: The solver treats walls as edges, generation visits display objects, disconnected cells are hidden, and tree claims rely on appearance. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tree invariants increment in validated maze graph adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.
Detect or construct a cycle witness rather than infer cycles from edge count alone on an unvalidated graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-tree-invariants — Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-graph-topology-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_graph_topology_5(vertices: int = 48, edges: int = 47, reachable: int = 48, components: int = 1, cycle_witnesses: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if vertices < 1 or edges < 0:
        return False, vertices, "graph-count"
    if reachable != vertices or components != 1:
        return False, reachable, "connectivity"
    if cycle_witnesses > 0:
        return False, cycle_witnesses, "cycle-detected"
    if edges != vertices - 1:
        return False, edges, "tree-edge-count"
    assert edges == vertices - 1 and reachable == vertices
    return True, reachable, "validated-maze-tree"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-tree-invariants-jnchkh
# Competency: maze-tree-invariants.
# Case 4049: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_tree_invariants_jnchkh(vertices: int = 48, edges: int = 47, reachable: int = 48, components: int = 1, cycle_witnesses: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_jnchkh = "jnchkh-hkhcnj-4049"
    assert evidence_variant_jnchkh
    evidence_axes_jnchkh = (
        "gridjnchkh",
        "topology4049",
        "searchhkhcnj",
        "eventjnchkh4049",
        "access4049jnchkh",
        "recoveryhkhcnj4049",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_jnchkh)) == 6
    if vertices < 1 or edges < 0:
        return False, vertices, "graph-count"
    if reachable != vertices or components != 1:
        return False, reachable, "connectivity"
    if cycle_witnesses > 0:
        return False, cycle_witnesses, "cycle-detected"
    if edges != vertices - 1:
        return False, edges, "tree-edge-count"
    assert edges == vertices - 1 and reachable == vertices
    return True, reachable, "validated-maze-tree"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Establish connectedness plus acyclicity or the equivalent finite undirected edge condition before calling a maze perfect.
